items.title: Warren Buffett Sold $133 Billion Worth of Stocks This Year: Here Are 2 He's Not Selling
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has been selling stocks, including Apple and Bank of America, but has not sold Coca-Cola or American Express in over 25 years. Coca-Cola and American Express are seen as attractive investments due to their strong brands and consistent performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Coca-Cola is one of Berkshire's longest-held investments, with Buffett valuing the company's strong brand and consistent revenue and profits. The stock's attractive dividend yield and growth opportunities make it a tempting buy on the dip.

Berkshire has held American Express for 30 years, as Buffett favors the company's strong brand power and its spend-centric business model that benefits from the growth of the economy. The company's recent success with card refreshes and new customer acquisitions make it a long-term growth prospect.

Berkshire has been selling its largest holding, Apple, as part of its higher-than-usual stock sales this year, reflecting Buffett's view that the market is overvalued and limiting the pool of quality businesses selling at sensible prices.
Berkshire has also trimmed its stake in Bank of America, another sizable investment for the company in recent years, as part of its stock sales.
